item.title: Malaysia Secures Fuel Supply Through August, Malay Mail Reports
item.description: Malaysia has secured fuel supply through August despite disruptions to energy routes, supported by continued access to the Strait of Hormuz, Malay Mail reported, citing Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim.

item.title: Japan Set to Secure More Oil Imports in May After April Slump
item.description: Japan’s May crude oil imports are expected to rebound to around 1.7 million barrels a day, roughly three-quarters of the level a year earlier, as refiners step up alternative procurement while the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed.

item.title: Palm Fruit Left to Rot as Indonesia Export Revamp Hits Farmers
item.description: Some palm oil refiners in Indonesia are avoiding buying fruit from small farmers, a sign of how the government’s commodity export overhaul risks squeezing grower incomes and crimping supplies of the crop.

item.title: Hong Kong-Listed Biotech Insilico Is Said to Explore UAE Listing
item.description: Hong Kong-listed biotech firm Insilico Medicine Cayman TopCo is considering a secondary listing in Abu Dhabi, people familiar with the matter said, in a deal that would make it the first company from outside the Gulf to list shares on a regional exchange.

item.title: Indonesia Probes Major Palm Oil Companies on Export Price Issues
item.description: Indonesia is probing some of the world’s biggest palm-oil companies for suspected export abuses, adding to the upheaval in the industry days after the government announced a plan to nationalize shipments of the crop.

item.title: China Expands Travel Curbs to Top AI Talent at Private Firms
item.description: China is restricting overseas travel for top AI professionals in private firms such as Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. and DeepSeek, suggesting an escalation in measures intended to safeguard its technology and catch up to the US in a pivotal sphere.

item.title: Japan Sends Officials to Russia to ‘Facilitate Communication’
item.description: Japan has sent senior officials to Russia to keep communication channels open and protect the assets of Japanese companies operating there, in what Tokyo says is the latest in a series of visits that have taken place since the war in Ukraine began.

item.title: Danish Central Bank Warns of Housing Risks as Price Gains Spread
item.description: A surge in Copenhagen home prices is spilling over to the rest of Denmark, posing growing risks to the country’s lenders, the central bank said.

item.title: Deadly Heat Wave Breaks May Records Across Northwest Europe
item.description: The first major heat wave of the season broke temperature records across northwest Europe, triggered water shortages in the UK and has been linked to several deaths in France.

item.title: ING’s ‘Vibe Coding’ AI Is Building Its New Trading Systems
item.description: ING Groep NV is turning to “vibe coding” to build electronic trading tools for currencies and credit, its latest use of artificial intelligence to compete with larger peers.

item.title: Hedge Funds Backing Futu, Up Fintech Hit by China Crackdown
item.description: China’s crackdown on cross-border trading sent the shares of popular brokers Futu Holdings Ltd. and Up Fintech Holding Ltd. plummeting, threatening pain for hedge funds and other investors who back these firms.

item.title: Ethio Telecom Lists on Bourse, Marking Ethiopia’s First IPO
item.description: Ethio telecom listed on Ethiopia’s nascent stock market more than a year since the close of its initial public offering and marking the nation’s maiden IPO.

item.title: European Stocks Steady as Traders Watch Iran War; Ferrari Drops
item.description: European stocks were muted after six days of gains as traders monitored the latest tensions in the Middle East for signs the US and Iran are nearing a deal.

item.title: Malaysia Jolts Bullion Trade With 10% Import Duty on Gold Bars
item.description: Malaysia has imposed a 10% import duty on some gold bar shipments, according to traders familiar with the matter, disrupting the bullion trade in the Southeast Asian nation.

item.title: China Lets Policy Loan Rate Fall to Record Low to Boost Economy
item.description: China let the interest rate on a one-year policy loan to banks decline to a record low, according to people familiar with the matter, a sign Beijing is stepping up efforts to support an economy that’s losing momentum.

item.title: ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ Studio Draws Tencent Music Investment
item.description: The Black Label Inc., the studio behind the soundtrack for Netflix Inc.’s KPop Demon Hunters, raised about 120 billion won ($80 million) of expansion capital from major entertainment names including Tencent Holdings Ltd.’s music arm.

item.title: Bitcoin Volatility Hits Nine-Month Low as Crypto Takes Breather
item.description: Bitcoin’s expected volatility has fallen to the lowest level in nine months, as subdued trading and a shift in speculative interest away from the largest cryptocurrency dampen demand for options protection.

item.title: RBI, Rating Firms Assess Iran War Risks on Indian Companies
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India has held discussions with local credit-rating companies to gauge potential stress among borrowers caused by the US-Iran war, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: S. Korea to Deploy Locally Built Nuclear Submarine by Late-2030s
item.description: South Korea unveiled a roadmap to develop nuclear-powered submarines and deploy the first vessel by the late 2030s, as President Lee Jae Myung convened a new defense strategy council Tuesday focused on strengthening the nation’s military autonomy.

item.title: A Gold Miner Is Set to Reboot a Hong Kong Listing Type Not Seen in 12 Years
item.description: Indonesian gold miner PT Merdeka Gold Resources is gearing up for a Hong Kong stock-market debut with a listing structure that’s fallen out of favor for more than a decade, a sign of the city’s booming market for fundraising drumming up interest across Asia and beyond.

item.title: FTSE 100, Gilts Make Up Ground After Long Weekend
ECB Should Hike Interest Rates in June, Schnabel Says
item.description: The European Central Bank should raise interest rates next month even if there’s a quick resolution to the conflict in the Middle East, according to Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.

item.title: Samsung’s Non-Chip Staff Seek Court Order to Block Wage Vote
item.description: A Samsung Electronics Co. union representing workers outside the ultra-profitable semiconductor division asked a Korean court to block voting on a tentative deal that would distribute about 40 trillion won ($26.6 billion) in bonuses to chip employees.

item.title: Chip Bonus Boom Raises Inflation, Housing Risks for BOK, BE Says
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co.’s bonus deal with workers risks turning South Korea’s AI-driven chip boom into a new source of inflation and housing-market pressure that the Bank of Korea will have to address, according to Bloomberg Economics.

item.title: Sasol’s Blistering Rally Meets Growing Skepticism From Analysts
item.description: After a rally that’s seen Sasol Ltd.’s stock price double this year, analysts are becoming wary of the South African oil and chemicals company.

item.title: RedNote-Backer GSR Seeks to Raise $350 Million New Fund
item.description: GSR Ventures Management Co., an early investor in Chinese social media app RedNote, is seeking investors for a new fund, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: The Pope Speaks Up for Humans, not Humanoids
item.description: The forces driving the threat of robot overlords get a morality check.

item.title: Korea to Monitor $37 Billion of Overseas Private Credit Exposure
item.description: South Korea will monitor overseas private debt investments from the nation that total some $37 billion, the government said, following recent scares in the industry globally.

item.title: Hungary Poised to Hold Key Rate With June Cut Seen in Play
item.description: Hungary’s central bank is expected to keep the key interest rate unchanged at the second-highest level in the European Union before potentially weighing monetary easing next month.

item.title: Iran War Boosts $10 Billion Kazakh Rail Plan Linking EU to China
item.description: Kazakhstan’s national railroad operator is expanding its track and infrastructure to meet growing demand for transporting goods between China and Europe as trade flows shift in response to the Iran war.
